examples: Thousands of penguins have already died from a shortage of krill.
Krill fishing in the Antarctic has worsened the pollution of the pristine waters.
Shrinking krill population and rising temperatures could force Antarctic whales to migrate.
Once heavily hunted, humpback whales that feed on krill have made a comeback in Antarctic waters.
"We hope to look at the densities of the krill that the penguins are exploiting, " Dr Trathan explained.
